reaches.name: Boise River - In Town
reaches.gageNotes: Boise River at Glenwood Bridge is the best representative in-town Boise River gage. Ignore nearby IDWSC calibration pseudo-site.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Urban walk-wade reach. Moderate flows are best; high irrigation releases reduce wade access and low flows can warm quickly.

reaches.name: Boise River - South Fork
reaches.gageNotes: SF Boise River at Anderson Ranch Dam is closest to the tailwater release. Neal Bridge can provide downstream context closer to Arrowrock.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Cold tailwater. It can fish well at higher flows than small freestones, especially from a boat, but wading becomes limited as releases rise.

reaches.name: Boise River - Middle Fork
reaches.gageNotes: Boise River near Twin Springs provides same-river upper Boise drainage context for the Middle Fork/Atlanta decision. Treat as representative enough for MVP flow scoring, but validate against local Middle Fork access and runoff observations.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Remote mountain freestone. Fishability depends heavily on runoff, clarity, and road access; Twin Springs flow is useful upper-drainage context but still needs local validation for the exact Middle Fork reach.

reaches.name: Henry's Fork - Box Canyon
reaches.gageNotes: Henrys Fork near Island Park is the closest likely release/Box Canyon representative. Confirm relation to exact Box Canyon section.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Tailwater canyon water. Productive at stronger releases than meadow sections; very low flows reduce habitat and very high releases make presentation and wading harder.

reaches.name: Henry's Fork - Harriman Ranch
reaches.gageNotes: Island Park gage is upstream context; Henrys Fork near Ashton provides downstream context. Ranch-specific interpretation may need both.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Technical meadow dry-fly water. Stable, moderate flows are more important than raw volume; wind and hatch timing often decide the day.

reaches.name: Snake River - South Fork Canyon
reaches.gageNotes: Snake River near Irwin is the closest South Fork canyon candidate; Snake River near Heise provides downstream context.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Large tailwater float reach. It fishes well at volumes that would be too high for smaller rivers; wind, releases, and boat control matter more than wading comfort.

reaches.name: Big Wood - Ketchum
reaches.gageNotes: Big Wood River at Ketchum is geographically ideal but did not return current IV flow. Big Wood River at Hailey total flow is the best live-flow context for now; Stanton Crossing provides lower-valley context. This reach still needs local validation.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Medium freestone walk-wade river. Lower summer flows can fish well if temperatures stay cool; high runoff quickly limits safe wading and clarity.

reaches.name: Silver Creek - Preserve
reaches.gageNotes: Silver Creek at Sportsman Access near Picabo is the clearest named Silver Creek gage; West Campground may provide additional local context.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Spring creek. Stable clarity, wind, hatch timing, and temperature matter more than high volume; very low warm flows should trigger caution.

reaches.name: Teton River - Driggs/Victor
reaches.gageNotes: Teton River above South Leigh Creek near Driggs is the nearest same-river candidate for the Driggs/Victor reach.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Valley freestone/meadow water. Clarity and storm pulses are major constraints; moderate flows are best for mixed wade and float options.

reaches.name: Salmon River - Upper Stanley
reaches.gageNotes: Needs manual review. Nearest candidate is Valley Creek, which is not the Salmon River. Salmon River below Yankee Fork is downstream context, not necessarily representative of Upper Stanley.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Cold mountain freestone. Runoff and water temperature dominate; range is a placeholder until a representative Upper Stanley gage is verified.

reaches.name: Clearwater - Middle Fork
reaches.gageNotes: Needs manual review. Candidate list includes South Fork Clearwater, Lawyer Creek, Upper Clear Creek, Lolo Creek, and Lochsa River; none is a clean Middle Fork Clearwater at Kooskia match.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Larger North Idaho freestone system. It can fish at higher volume than small walk-wade rivers, but clarity and tributary influence need local confirmation.

reaches.name: St. Joe - Upper
reaches.gageNotes: St. Joe River at Calder is the nearest same-river candidate, though it may be downstream of the upper reach. Treat as contextual until section is refined.

conditionRules.flowProfile: Large cutthroat freestone. Falling post-runoff flows are often the key signal; high water can limit wading even when fish are active.
